info.title: Blink Markets API
info.description: Trading API for Blink Prediction Markets

get.summary: Health check - GET /

post.summary: Create new API credentials (L1 EIP-712 auth)
post.description:
POST /auth/api-key

Headers:
- BLINK-ADDRESS: Wallet address
- BLINK-SIGNATURE: EIP-712 signature
- BLINK-TIMESTAMP: Unix timestamp
- BLINK-NONCE: Random nonce

get.summary: Derive existing API credentials (L1 EIP-712 auth)
get.description:
GET /auth/derive-api-key

If credentials exist, returns them. Otherwise returns 404.
py-clob-client's create_or_derive_api_creds() tries this first,
then falls back to POST /auth/api-key if not found.

404.description: No valid credentials found for this wallet

get.summary: Get balance and allowance for a user - GET /balance-allowance
get.description:
Query params:
- asset_type: "COLLATERAL" (USDC) or "CONDITIONAL" (CTF tokens)
- token_id: Required for CONDITIONAL type, the token ID to check

200.description: Balance and allowance for the asset
Orderbook with bids, asks, and metadata

get.summary: Get unified orderbook - GET /book/unified
get.description:
Returns both direct and implied liquidity (from cross-book MINT/MERGE opportunities)
with visual distinction between sources.

200.description: Unified orderbook with direct and implied liquidity

get.summary: Get single order - GET /data/order/:id

200.description: User's open orders with pagination cursor
User's trade history with pagination cursor

post.summary: POST /faucet/claim — mint TestUSDC to a user address

get.summary: Liveness probe - just checks if the process is running
get.description:
GET /health

Returns 200 if the process is alive, 503 if not.
This is a lightweight check that doesn't verify dependencies.

get.summary: Readiness probe - checks if the service can handle traffic
get.description:
GET /health/ready

Returns 200 if ready to serve traffic, 503 if not.
Checks critical dependencies: database, blockchain RPC.

200.description: Most recent trade price for the token

get.summary: List markets

parameters.name: include_resolved
parameters.description: Set to "true" to include resolved markets (default: active only)

get.summary: Get market by ID
Get order book for a market
get.description:
Query params:
- `token_id`: Optional hex string (no 0x prefix). Defaults to YES token.

Examples:
- `GET /markets/:id/orderbook` → YES token orderbook
- `GET /markets/:id/orderbook?token_id=<hex>` → Specific token orderbook

parameters.name: token_id
parameters.description: Optional token_id (hex string, no 0x prefix). Defaults to YES token.

200.description: Midpoint price between best bid and ask

delete.description:
Accepts both:
- JSON body: {"orderID": "..."} or {"order_id": "..."} (Polymarket client)
- Query param: ?order_id=... (backward compatibility)

get.summary: List orders with filters

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of orders to return

delete.summary: Cancel multiple orders by ID - DELETE /orders
delete.description: SDK sends JSON array of order ID strings: ["id1", "id2", ...]

requestBody.description: Array of order ID strings to cancel

get.summary: Get order by ID

200.description: Best price for the given side
Spread between best bid and ask
Minimum tick size and order size
Current server time as Unix seconds

get.summary: Get user profile summary (public)
GET /users/:address/activity — unified activity feed
Get user's on-chain history from blockchain indexer
get.description:
Returns all indexed on-chain events (fills, transfers, redemptions) for a user.
This is the authoritative history — derived directly from blockchain events.

GET /users/:address/history

get.summary: Get user orders from DATABASE (our matching engine state)
get.description:
Orders are off-chain until matched, so we query our database.

GET /users/:address/orders

get.summary: GET /users/:address/portfolio-history — portfolio milestones for frontend chart
get.description:
Returns position-changing events (buy/sell/mint/redemption) with running USDC balance.
The frontend combines these milestones with live position valuations to render
a smooth portfolio chart.

get.summary: GET /users/:address/positions — on-chain CTF token balances for active markets.
get.description:
Returns non-zero positions only. Public endpoint (on-chain data).
Uses the existing ctf_balance_cache (5s TTL) to avoid redundant RPC calls.

200.description: CTF token positions for active markets

get.summary: Get user redemption history from DATABASE
get.description:
Returns redemptions based on settled trades for resolved markets.
We track settlements in our DB.

GET /users/:address/redemptions

get.summary: Get user trades from DATABASE (our matching engine history)

post.summary: POST /v1/permit — relay a signed EIP-2612 permit to set USDC allowance
post.description:
The user signs the permit off-chain (no gas). This endpoint submits it
on-chain using the operator/faucet wallet (which pays gas).
This enables gasless USDC approvals for users.

400.description: Invalid parameters or spender not exchange contract

post.summary: POST /v1/prefund-ctf-approval — sponsor ETH so user can call setApprovalForAll
post.description:
1. Check if CTF is already approved → return early
2. Check if user has enough ETH → return early
3. Send ETH from faucet wallet, wait for receipt
4. Return confirmation so frontend can immediately send the approval TX

200.description: Prefund result (may already be approved or funded)

429.description: Rate limited — 1 prefund per address per 24 hours

get.summary: GET /wallet-status?address=0x...
get.description:
Returns USDC balance, USDC allowance (for exchange), and CTF approval status
for the given wallet address. All values are publicly readable on-chain, so
no authentication is required.

Backend caches these with 5s TTL, eliminating redundant RPC calls when
multiple browser tabs or users query the same address.

200.description: Wallet USDC balance, allowance, CTF approval, and ETH balance

BookResponse.description:
Get orderbook - GET /book
Polymarket-compatible response with all expected fields

CancelOrderBody.description: Cancel order body (Polymarket client sends JSON body)

MakerOrderInfo.description: Polymarket-compatible maker order info (embedded in trades)

OrderInfo.description: Order info returned by /data/orders - matches Polymarket PolymarketOpenOrder format for Nautilus

asset_id.description: Token ID as decimal string (for int() conversion)

associate_trades.description: Associate trades (list of trade IDs or null)

expiration.description: Expiration timestamp in ms (as string or null) - must always be present

status.description: Status in UPPERCASE for Nautilus Trader compatibility: LIVE, MATCHED, CANCELED, etc.

asks.description: Ask (sell) orders sorted by price ascending

bids.description: Bid (buy) orders sorted by price descending

token_id.description: Token ID this orderbook is for

OrdersResponse.description:
Get user's open orders - GET /data/orders
Response format matches Polymarket py-clob-client expectations

available_usdc.description: Engine's available USDC after holds (defense-in-depth, also in wallet-status)

data.description: Array of order data (Polymarket uses 'data' not 'orders')

next_cursor.description: Pagination cursor - "LTE=" means end of results (base64 "-1")

TradeInfo.description:
Polymarket-compatible trade info (PolymarketTradeReport format)
Required fields for Nautilus Trader: taker_order_id, maker_address, transaction_hash, maker_orders, trader_side

fee_rate_bps.description: Fee rate in basis points (required by Nautilus)

last_update.description: Last update timestamp (required by Nautilus Trader)

taker_order_id.description: Required by Nautilus - order ID of the taker

order_id.description: Order ID that generated this trade

TradesResponse.description:
Get user's trades - GET /data/trades
Matches Polymarket py-clob-client format (uses 'data' not 'trades')

next_cursor.description: Pagination cursor for next page (Polymarket compatibility)

UnifiedPriceLevel.description: Unified price level showing both direct and implied liquidity

eth_balance.description:
ETH balance for gas (raw wei as string). Included so SDK consumers
never need direct RPC access for any balance check.
